description: Coalossal is fire/rock and double weak to both water and ground, and Gogoat covers exactly those threats — it dominates 'the mud boys' (e.g. Quagsire), tanking Stone Edge and Aqua Tail while farming down with Vine Whips. The creator highlights this Coalossal + grass pairing as the well-working backbone of the team.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: COALOSSAL brings HUGE BULK to Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Both serve as the two safe swaps backing Swampert, answering its grass weakness and offering generally safe matchups across the limited meta.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT Makes Sunshine Cup EASY | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Recurring backbone of the confusion-spam teams, paired with a confusion user (Oranguru or Shadow Girafarig) in the middle slot.

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Sunshine Cup | Pokemon GO PvP

description: Oranguru pairs really well with Cradily's weaknesses — the creator intentionally put them together, with Oranguru's safety and lack of hard answers covering what the grass-type Cradily lead struggles against.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

description: The two staple Pokémon of the cup — the creator says both belong on nearly every team. Quagsire beats normal, fire, ground, and grass; Cradily is the #1 ranked Pokémon.

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Sunshine Cup | Pokemon GO PvP

description: Swampert's Hydro Cannon can out-spam many threats; the creator stands with Swampert and pivots into Cradily as a half-sacrifice. Cradily also covers Swampert's lone grass weakness, making them a safe pivot pairing.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT Makes Sunshine Cup EASY | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Fire Ninetales pairs with the Mud Boys to patch their grass weakness, while Seismitoad acts as the safe swap also carrying Icy Wind to deal with grass like Cradily and Abomasnow.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: The MUDBOYS RULE the Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: The 'double Mud Boy' core — two bulky ground-types that feel very safe in the meta, resisting fire and key spam while sharing super-effective ground/water coverage. Their shared grass weakness is intentionally covered by the Ninetales lead.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: The MUDBOYS RULE the Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: The two backline Pokémon cover each other's weaknesses — Toucannon's flying/normal offense pairs with Quagsire's water/ground bulk and Aqua Tail spam, giving the team coverage behind the glassy Blaziken lead.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: BLAZIKEN: The Glass Cannon Tearing Through Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO

description: Talonflame and Seismitoad cover each other's double weaknesses: Talonflame's fire beats the grass that is double super-effective on Seismitoad, and Seismitoad's ground beats the rock that is double super-effective on Talonflame.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: I FINALLY got my STARTING ELO | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Toucannon (flyer) and Swampert (ground 'mud boy' only weak to grass) cover each other's weaknesses — Toucannon deals with the grass types that threaten Swampert, while Swampert covers ground/rock matchups like Coalossal that Toucannon loses to.
